behavior_in_task_description.reason: Tests assert specific file paths like 'pkg/compose/publish.go' and 'pkg/e2e/compose_run_build_once_test.go', exact counts (>= 3 fmt.Fprintf calls), exact strings like 'make build', 'go test', 'golangci-lint', '.golangci.yml', 'fmt.Fprintf'. While the instruction mentions many of these concepts, it never names the specific files 'pkg/compose/publish.go' or 'pkg/e2e/compose_run_build_once_test.go'. The agent must discover these files on its own. Also, the test_publish_uses_fprintf asserts >= 3 fmt

no_solution_leakage.reason: The instruction describes what's broken (anti-pattern, unnecessary nolint directive, missing CLAUDE.md) but does not specify exact line numbers or the precise replacement code. The agent must find and fix the issues themselves.

solution_uniqueness_guard.reason: The tests check for absence of the anti-pattern (WriteString(fmt.Sprintf)), presence of fmt.Fprintf (>=3 calls), absence of //nolint:errcheck, and CLAUDE.md containing specific keywords. Any correct fix replacing the anti-pattern with fmt.Fprintf would pass. The CLAUDE.md content checks are for keywords, not exact text. Alternative correct fixes would generally pass.

tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: The test suite includes execution-based tests: test_go_syntax_valid runs gofmt, test_go_vet_compose runs go vet, test_unit_test_publish runs go test, test_go_build runs go build, test_unit_tests_compose runs go test. While some tests (test_publish_uses_fprintf, test_no_unnecessary_nolint, test_claude_md_*) do text-based checks, the majority of tests execute the code. Not every test is purely text-based.

test_not_tautological.reason: The fail-to-pass tests (test_publish_uses_fprintf, test_no_unnecessary_nolint, test_claude_md_documents_build, test_claude_md_documents_lint_style) require actual changes: replacing WriteString(fmt.Sprintf with fmt.Fprintf, removing //nolint:errcheck, and creating CLAUDE.md with specific content. A stub or no-op would not pass these tests.

behavior_in_tests.reason: All behaviors required by instruction.md are tested: elimination of WriteString(fmt.Sprintf) anti-pattern (test_publish_uses_fprintf), removal of //nolint:errcheck (test_no_unnecessary_nolint), CLAUDE.md creation with required content (test_claude_md_documents_build, test_claude_md_documents_lint_style), and existing tests passing (test_unit_tests_compose, test_go_build, etc.).

informative_test_structure.reason: Tests are well-organized with clear section comments ('Gates (pass_to_pass, static)', 'Fail-to-pass (pr_diff) — core code style tests', 'Fail-to-pass (pr_diff) — config/documentation update tests'), descriptive function names, and docstrings explaining each test's purpose.

structured_data_schema.reason: The task does not produce structured data like JSON/CSV/API/DB outputs.

no_typos.reason: No typos found in filenames, paths, commands, or variable names in the instruction or test files.

instruction_no_hint_leakage.reason: The instruction describes the anti-patterns and issues conceptually without naming specific files or line numbers. It says 'Find all instances' and 'One of the Go test files', requiring the agent to locate the relevant code.

config_edit_scope_bounded.reason: The gold patch is localized: targeted line changes in publish.go (3 substitutions), one line removal in the test file, creation of a new CLAUDE.md, and a minor Dockerfile version bump. No full-file rewrites.
